title: betula pubescens var. pumila
intro: Betula pubescens var. pumila, also known as dwarf birch, is a shrub or small tree species of the birch family. It is native to northern Europe, where it is valued for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. The bark of the dwarf birch contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and bark can be used to make herbal preparations such as tea, decoctions, and infusions, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, offering health benefits including reduced inflammation and pain relief.
